steps.title: Backsolving — testing choice B
steps.content:
Backsolving — testing choice B (0.51x):
If the resulting number were $$0.51x$$, the increase would be $$0.51x - x = -0.49x$$, which is a $$-49\%$$ change (a decrease).
This does not match a 149% increase.

steps.title: Testing choice C
steps.content:
Fails. Testing choice C (1.49x):
Increase = $$1.49x - x = 0.49x$$ => $$49\%$$ increase.
Still not 149%.

steps.title: Testing choice D
steps.content:
Testing choice D (2.49x):
Increase = $$2.49x - x = 1.49x$$ => $$149\%$$ increase.
This matches the given percent increase.
That matches choice D — D is correct.
